other_observers: Cole Wild, Tom ?; Reported at this location 21 October 2010 by Mark Sanchez; seen daily by many observers.
specific_location: Red Rocks Trading Post west feeder
description: Gray bird approximately size and shape of nearby Western Scrub-Jays feeding on the ground near bird-feeders; tail without noticeable markings, approximately as long as body; wings with barely noticeable wingbars formed by pale gray tips to a few greater coverts. Apparently missing pale gray-tipped coverts may indicate active molt. Pale gray underparts heavily spotted with large dark-gray, roundish spots, heaviest on upper breast, fading on lower breast and flanks; belly becoming salmon-colored, heaviest on vent. Throat clear white with thin dark gray malar stripe, white malar with remainder of head cold gray as the upperparts (back, wings, tail). Iris orange. Bill long (2/3 or 3/4 as long as the width of the head from bill to hindcrown). Bill black with possibly paler base of both mandible and maxilla. Maxilla strongly curved; mandible weakly curved. Legs and feet black.Behaviors: Hopping on ground feeding; territorial, chasing off other birds.Call: nonePlumage: Adult
photographer: Photographer: N Komar, N Komar, N Komar, N Komar, N Komar
